first_impression_why: A immediately communicates a specific product with a clear use case, while B feels like a generic social app landing page that could be any number of consumer startups.
reasoning: A wins because it reads as a real, traction-backed product company — you can see downloads, stars, and understand exactly what it does within seconds. B has more visual energy and design polish (bright colors, photography), but the value proposition is vague and there are no visible traction metrics. As a VC, A gives you something concrete to evaluate; B gives you vibes.
